page: Amazon Web Services g6.xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs, which correspond to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, and a local 250 GB NVMe SSD. It includes a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. The single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, while multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25%. However, the integrated NVIDIA L4 GPU delivers exceptional performance for machine learning tasks. Large language model inference speeds for both prompt processing and text generation using small (135M) and medium (7B) models rank in the top tier, placing in the top 10% of tested configurations. Conversely, general database operations and static web serving throughput are weak.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the g6.xlarge offers a specialized hardware profile that trades high multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance for top-tier GPU acceleration. This makes it a practical choice for workloads that can offload computation to the GPU rather than relying on heavy CPU-bound multi-threaded processing. The instance is highly suited for low-latency LLM inference, graphics-intensive applications, and machine learning workloads utilizing small to medium models, whereas it is less optimal for high-throughput web serving or memory-intensive database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services g6.xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual machine powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, a 250 GB NVMe SSD, and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The hardware is equipped with a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU featuring 22 GB of VRAM. While benchmarks indicate weak multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, the instance excels in GPU-accelerated tasks. Specifically, LLM inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation with 135M and 7B models rank in the top 10%. This hardware profile is highly optimized for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and GPU-driven workloads, offering a specialized balance of dedicated compute and high-performance acceleration rather than general-purpose multi-threaded CPU power.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6.xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, a 250 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB VRAM for top-tier LLM inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, optimized for LLM inference.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual machine featuring an NVIDIA L4 GPU and local NVMe storage, optimized for small to medium model inference.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 CPU with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB DDR4 RAM
Equipped with one NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU featuring 22 GB of VRAM
Includes 250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers top-tier benchmark performance for small and medium LLM inference
Best suited for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and GPU-bound workloads
